cityDNA.coreFantasy: A forest that became a stagfolk civilization through sacred groves, antlered halls, temple processions, public works, guild craft, hanging gardens, and woodland estates.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Elarion Thicket is not a city built in a forest; it is civic life grown carefully inside a living ecosystem.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Elarion Thicket turns groves, roots, antlers, temple rites, public surveys, guild craft, manor gardens, and sacred woodland memory into an ancient stewardship civilization.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Living wood, carved timber, mossed stone, root-wrapped masonry, antler arches, lantern paths, canopy halls, hanging gardens, blue civic lamps, woven banners, temple platforms, manor courts, and white-stag symbolism.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A stagfolk woodland civilization that values calm ownership, communal obligation, ecological memory, craft legitimacy, public stewardship, and territorial responsibility.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Antlers, white stags, roots, sacred groves, hanging gardens, lanterns, banners, canopy halls, mossed stone, and blue lamps symbolize place kept alive through care.

canonLocks: Elarion Thicket is a forest that gradually became a civilization, not a city imposed on woodland scenery.
The Wonder is the Antlered Root-Hearth of the White Stag.
The Beacon is the Sacred Grove Beacon.
The Seat of Power is the Antler Arches Sovereign Registry Court.
The five primary districts are Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Public Works Authority, Artisan Guilds, and High Residential.

The uploaded district anchors include Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Hanging Gardens Monument, Living Canopy Hall Nook, Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Temple Platform, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, Procession Stair Nook, Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, Guildhall, Woven Banners Monument, Masterwork Arcade Nook, High Residential Civic Hall, Manor Court, and Salon Pavilion Nook.
provisionalCanon: Elarion Thicket's highest civic virtue is stewardship.
The central tension is settlement versus ecosystem balance.
Hanging Gardens Monument appearing in both sacred grove and high residential contexts suggests cultivated beauty is also a territorial claim.
rejectedReads: Elarion Thicket is a primitive woodland village.
Elarion Thicket is wilderness with decorations instead of a mature city.
The stagfolk are passive nature spirits rather than territorial civic people.
Guilds and public works can be treated as generic urban features.
cautionFlags: Do not separate the city from the forest; the forest is civic infrastructure.
Keep sophistication visible through registry courts, guilds, public works, temples, and manor districts.
Use peaceful default behavior with territorial pride, not generic gentleness.
Preserve exact sidecar anchor names, especially Living Canopy Hall Nook, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, Masterwork Arcade Nook, and Salon Pavilion Nook.

environment.environmentalLogic: The forest is the city's road system, memory system, defensive cover, spiritual ground, and civic limit. Built forms grow only where the ecosystem can absorb them.

forms: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall
skyline: Antlered Root-Hearth of the White Stag as natural-civic wonder anchor
Antler Arches Sovereign Registry Court as central authority nested in forest form
Sacred Grove Beacon visible through trees, mist, and ridge gaps
guild halls, temple platforms, public works halls, and manor courts rising through canopy rather than dominating it

civilization.people: Citizens are stagfolk: serene, observant, communally rooted, territorial, craft-aware, and peaceful by default without being passive.
civilization.governance: Authority is distributed stewardship: registry law, grove rights, survey permits, guild obligations, public works, and manor influence govern how the forest-city grows.
civilization.religion: Religious life is grove-centered, white-stag symbolic, antler-marked, and inseparable from civic territory and seasonal stewardship rites.
customs: permit disputes over path and grove rights

streets.appearance: Woodland paths made civic over centuries: root-edged stone, timber porches, lanterns under branches, mossed steps, garden walls, guild fronts, clearings, antler arches, and canopy halls.
streets.movement: Movement follows paths, stairs, forest bridges, canopy walks, garden terraces, grove clearings, processional stairs, public works yards, and local shortcuts rather than straight urban grids.
sounds: soft hoof traffic on root-stone paths
streets.lighting: Daylight breaks through canopy and mist in patches; night is warm lanterns and blue civic lamps glowing beneath branches.
streets.weatherEffects: Rain darkens bark and stone, brightens moss, muffles the city, and makes lantern paths feel protected rather than wild.
streets.dayCycle: Mornings begin with mist, grove tending, path maintenance, public works surveys, guild stalls, and quiet registry business.
streets.nightCycle: Night is lanterned groves, muffled forest sound, closing guild stalls, blue civic lamps, temple processions, and protected paths.

housing.wealthy: Woodland manor courts with garden terraces, salon pavilions, carved antler lintels, private groves, hanging garden views, and lanterned approaches that remain integrated with the forest.
housing.middle: Guild homes, timber-and-stone houses, garden courtyards, branch-shadowed balconies, root foundations, and workshops beside civic paths.
housing.poor: Small root-edge dwellings, shared garden courts, mossed stone rooms, simple lantern porches, and compact homes near public works yards or path margins.

districts.name: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove
districts.civicPurpose: Spiritual-ecological core, public signal identity, grove memory, and shared stewardship focus.
districts.visualIdentity: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Sacred Grove Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Living Canopy Hall Nook, ancient trees, blue-green light, roots, lanterns, and quiet paths.
gameplayAssetNeeds: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall

districts.name: Religious / Temple Quarter
districts.civicPurpose: Ritual processions, seasonal rites, sacred teaching, and public ceremonial continuity.
districts.visualIdentity: Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Temple Platform, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, Procession Stair Nook, carved timber, antler details, mossed stone, and warm ritual light.

districts.name: Public Works Authority
districts.civicPurpose: Stewardship bureaucracy, path maintenance, surveys, permits, drainage, and civic growth control.
districts.visualIdentity: Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, maps, tools, root-stone boundaries, and practical civic yards.

districts.name: Artisan Guilds
districts.civicPurpose: Craft production, guild legitimacy, public making, and woodland trade.
districts.visualIdentity: Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, Guildhall, Woven Banners Monument, Masterwork Arcade Nook, carved stalls, woodcraft, lanterns, and market clearings.

districts.name: High Residential
districts.civicPurpose: Status, manor influence, domestic refinement, gardened hospitality, and quiet social hierarchy.
districts.visualIdentity: High Residential Civic Hall, Manor Court, Hanging Gardens Monument, Salon Pavilion Nook, garden terraces, lanterned porches, carved antler lintels, and private grove edges.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Refined, quiet, watchful, and socially territorial.

wonder.name: Antlered Root-Hearth of the White Stag
wonder.symbolicRole: Ancestry, territory, sacred grove, hearth, and woodland civilization made one living monument.
wonder.visualRole: Monumental root-and-antler structure integrated into canopy and earth, warmed by hearth light and marked by white-stag symbolism.
wonder.civicRole: Wonder-scale focus for seasonal rites, public gatherings, territorial oaths, coming-of-age observances, and memorial rituals.
storyHooks: A white-stag rite forces the city to choose between growth and stewardship.

beacon.name: Sacred Grove Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Warning, guidance, and stewardship rooted in living public space.
beacon.visualRole: Blue-green beacon nested among ancient trees, roots, lanterns, hanging gardens, and canopy platforms.
beacon.civicRole: Signal heart for grove response, public warnings, path orientation, lantern tending, and Conjunction readiness.
storyHooks: When the Beacon changes, the forest seems to notice before the people speak.

seatOfPower.name: Antler Arches Sovereign Registry Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Sovereignty, territory, lineage, and civic obligation recorded through antlered woodland law.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Formal civic court of antler arches, carved timber, mossed stone, registry halls, and controlled public approaches beneath ancient trees.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Government center for territorial claims, grove rights, public petitions, stewardship rulings, manor disputes, and registry proceedings.
storyHooks: A registry phrase changes who has the right to cut, cross, or defend a grove.

majorLandmarks.name: Temple Platform
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Raised timber-and-stone platform, lanterns, procession stairs, antler carvings, and mossed steps.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Public rites, seasonal ceremonies, sacred teaching, and communal observance.
storyHooks: A procession stops at the wrong stair and reveals hidden civic dissent.

majorLandmarks.name: Survey Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Timber civic hall with maps, blue lamps, survey tools, permit yards, and root-stone boundary markers.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Public works authority for paths, drainage, grove rights, building permissions, and ecological limits.
storyHooks: A survey map proves the forest has been protecting an illegal route.

majorLandmarks.name: Guildhall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Woodland guildhall with woven banners, carved timber, masterwork arcades, lanterns, and craft displays.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Guild certification, artisan trade, public making, and craft politics.
storyHooks: A masterwork banner encodes an old grove claim.

majorLandmarks.name: Manor Court
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Gardened manor court with pavilions, carved antler lintels, private grove edges, lantern porches, and refined paths.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: High residential influence, domestic hospitality, class negotiation, and quiet patronage.
storyHooks: A manor garden hides the political cost of keeping the forest beautiful.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Sacred Grove Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Living Canopy Hall Nook, ancient trees, roots, and lantern paths.
S2 must show Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Temple Platform, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, Procession Stair Nook, ritual banners, and temple attendants.
S3 must show Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, survey tools, maps, and path workers.
S4 must show Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, Guildhall, Woven Banners Monument, Masterwork Arcade Nook, woodcarvers, weavers, and craft displays.
S5 must show High Residential Civic Hall, Manor Court, Hanging Gardens Monument, Salon Pavilion Nook, garden terraces, manor paths, and quiet social hierarchy.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Use root-stone paths, living canopy, antler arches, mossed stone, lanterns, woven banners, blue civic lamps, hanging gardens, temple platforms, guild halls, and forest-integrated public buildings.
The Antlered Root-Hearth of the White Stag must read as wonder; the Antler Arches Sovereign Registry Court must read as authority; the Sacred Grove Beacon must be visually embedded in living forest.
Show civic sophistication without separating city from ecosystem.
requiredVisualElements: Antlered Root-Hearth of the White Stag
Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall
elementsToAvoid: forest backdrop separated from civic structure
palace architecture that ignores root and canopy logic
